name: Shimane Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the iron-rich earth and historic ironworks of Shimane

description: A deep-crimson field charged at the centre with a yellow emblem composed of four stylised mountain shapes arranged in a circular cross-form.
history: Shimane's prefectural symbol was adopted on 8 March 1968. The flag, with the yellow emblem on a maroon field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: deep crimson, recalling the iron-rich earth of the San'in region and the historic ironworking traditions of Shimane

features.role: Shimane prefectural emblem (four stylised kanji 山)
features.symbolism: four stylised kanji 山 ('mountain') arranged in a circular cross-shape, evoking the kanji 雲 of 出雲 (Izumo, the historic name of the region) and representing the four points of the compass and the unity of the prefecture's regions; the design also recalls the kanji 島 ('island') in 島根 (Shimane).
